content: By 2025, autonomous self-supervised agents can be redesigned to leverage a recursive 'agent within agent' memory architecture, where nested sub-agents independently curate and self-supervise on different slices of the main agent’s environmental experience, rather than relying on monolithic memory systems. This nested structure eliminates the forgetting plaguing single-agent curiosity-driven learning by letting specialized sub-agents retain niche exploratory knowledge without overwriting the main agent’s task-critical memory.
